step_log.notes: SSDT GreenAi.DB.sqlproj fails on dotnet CLI (pre-existing, requires VS MSBuild) — not caused by this step
Additive only — method not yet called in handler. UserMembershipRecord uses int fields (consistent with existing Dapper records, no TypeHandler registered).
GetMembershipsAsync mock added to both success-path tests. Failure-path tests require no setup — handler exits before membership call.

step_log.description: Refactor LoginHandler to use membership model
notes: LoginHandler: GetMembershipsAsync called post-password-validation. 0 memberships→ACCOUNT_HAS_NO_TENANT, 1→JWT with membership.CustomerId, >1→RequiresCustomerSelection
LoginResponse: WithToken() and RequiresCustomerSelection() factories added. NeedsCustomerSelection bool discriminates cases. Non-nullable token fields preserve test compatibility.
LoginPage.razor: NeedsCustomerSelection guard added (TODO placeholder for customer selection page).
LanguageId read from membership.LanguageId but not yet embedded in JWT — deferred to Step 6 (GreenAiClaims + ICurrentUser).
JwtTokenService signature unchanged to avoid cascading changes to RefreshTokenHandler which has no LanguageId yet.

step_log.description: Add LanguageId to full auth flow (claim, ICurrentUser, JwtTokenService, LoginHandler, RefreshTokenHandler)
notes: GreenAiClaims.LanguageId = 'greenai:language_id' added
ICurrentUser.LanguageId: int property added
HttpContextCurrentUser: reads GreenAiClaims.LanguageId from principal
JwtTokenService.CreateToken: languageId int parameter added, embedded as claim
LoginHandler: passes membership.LanguageId to CreateToken
RefreshTokenHandler: passes languageId=1 (default) — gap: UserRefreshTokens has no LanguageId column yet, to be addressed in Step 7+
V006: ALTER TABLE UserRefreshTokens ADD LanguageId INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
LanguageId persisted at login time (membership.LanguageId), rotated through refresh flow (record.LanguageId)
LANGUAGE_PLACEMENT locked decision now fully honoured end-to-end — LanguageId originates in UserCustomerMembership at login, persisted through token rotation without recalculation
RefreshTokenRecord gains LanguageId field — Dapper column order updated in FindValidRefreshToken.sql

step_log.description: SelectCustomer vertical slice — multi-tenant customer selection
notes: POST /api/auth/select-customer — requires authentication (IRequireAuthentication marker + .RequireAuthorization())
Handler reads UserId from ICurrentUser (never from client), verifies membership in UserCustomerMembership, issues JWT with LanguageId from membership row
ICurrentUser.Email added (reads ClaimTypes.Email from principal) — needed by JwtTokenService.CreateToken
MembershipRecord: CustomerId, LanguageId, DefaultProfileId — DefaultProfileId from LEFT JOIN Profiles
FindMembership.sql filters UserId AND CustomerId AND IsActive=1
SelectCustomerRepository.SaveRefreshToken.sql is co-located — no cross-feature SQL dependency

step_log.description: Remove Users.CustomerId; rewrite FindUserByEmail.sql as pure identity query
notes: Users.CustomerId FK dropped (dynamic FK discovery) and column removed in V007 (idempotent)
FindUserByEmail.sql rewritten: pure identity query — no JOIN, no CustomerId, no DefaultProfileId
LoginUserRecord: CustomerId and DefaultProfileId removed; fields: Id, Email, PasswordHash, PasswordSalt, FailedLoginCount, IsLockedOut
LoginHandler: user.DefaultProfileId replaced with new ProfileId(0) placeholder — Profile decision deferred to Step 11
AuthTestDataBuilder.InsertUserAsync: customerId param removed; INSERT no longer writes CustomerId column
LoginRepositoryTests: 2 profile-dependent FindByEmail tests deleted, 1 identity-only test added (net -1 = 31 total)
FindValidRefreshToken.sql NOT changed — reads CustomerId from UserRefreshTokens (not Users) — unaffected
Test count: 31 (was 32) — expected delta from deleted obsolete profile tests

step_log.description: Fix test infrastructure gaps: LanguageId in InsertRefreshTokenAsync + InsertUserCustomerMembershipAsync
notes: InsertTokenOptions.LanguageId added (default 1) — INSERT now includes LanguageId column
InsertRefreshTokenAsync: UserRefreshTokens INSERT updated to include LanguageId — matches V006 schema
InsertUserCustomerMembershipAsync(UserId, CustomerId, int languageId=1) added — inserts into UserCustomerMembership with IsActive=1
No production code changes

step_log.description: Add full test coverage for SelectCustomer feature
notes: SelectCustomerHandlerTests: 6 tests — Handle_ValidMembership_ReturnsToken, Handle_ValidMembership_SavesRefreshTokenWithMembershipLanguageId, Handle_ValidMembership_UsesUserIdFromCurrentUser, Handle_MembershipNotFound_ReturnsError, Handle_InactiveMembership_ReturnsError, Handle_MembershipNotFound_DoesNotSaveRefreshToken
SelectCustomerRepositoryTests: 7 tests — FindMembership (active, no profile, inactive, unknown user, wrong customer) + SaveRefreshToken (persists languageId, token retrievable)
CreateHandler helper extracted — removes jwt/currentUser boilerplate from every handler test
Naming pattern: MethodName_StateUnderTest_ExpectedBehavior applied throughout
AuthTestDataBuilder: comment added above InsertUserCustomerMembershipAsync documenting that LanguageId=1 default is test-only — production must always pass explicit LanguageId
No production code changes

step_log.description: SelectProfile vertical slice — resolve ProfileId from Profiles table, eliminate ProfileId(0) placeholder from all auth flows
notes: Profile model (V009): many-to-many via ProfileUserMappings — Profiles.UserId FK removed. SUPERSEDED by Step 14 (Profile Hardening Phase 2.B).
V008: ALTER TABLE UserRefreshTokens ADD ProfileId INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — DEFAULT 0 covers pre-migration rows only. New application code must always supply a real ProfileId > 0.
SelectProfile endpoint: POST /api/auth/select-profile (.RequireAuthorization())
Handler: 0 profiles→PROFILE_NOT_FOUND, 1→auto-resolve, >1 no cmd.ProfileId→NeedsProfileSelection, explicit valid→select, explicit invalid→PROFILE_ACCESS_DENIED
ProfileId > 0 guaranteed before JwtTokenService.CreateToken in all three entry flows (Login, SelectCustomer, SelectProfile)
ISelectCustomerRepository.cs was stale (old signature) — fixed: added GetProfilesAsync + ProfileId param; duplicate interface removed from SelectCustomerRepository.cs
LoginRepositoryTests.SaveRefreshTokenAsync test updated to pass ProfileId (was missing required param)
SelectCustomerHandlerTests fully rewritten (8 tests incl. multi-profile + no-profile cases)
SelectCustomerRepositoryTests fully rewritten (removed DefaultProfileId COALESCE test, added GetProfilesAsync tests)
AuthTestDataBuilder.InsertTokenOptions gains ProfileId (default 0 for V008 migration path tests)

step_log.description: IRequireProfile marker + RequireProfileBehavior — enforce ProfileId > 0 at pipeline level
notes: IRequireProfile marker created in RequireProfileBehavior.cs (same file — mirrors IRequireAuthentication pattern)
RequireProfileBehavior<TRequest,TResponse>: blocks requests where ProfileId.Value <= 0, returns Result<T>.Fail('PROFILE_NOT_SELECTED'). Auth commands exempt (no IRequireProfile).
Pipeline order: LoggingBehavior → AuthorizationBehavior → RequireProfileBehavior → ValidationBehavior
ICurrentUser.cs: XML comments added documenting all runtime guarantees and forbidden states

13_VALIDATION_RULES.json: profile_required_before_protected_operations promoted from advisory_until_step_12 → mandatory. Added jwt_must_never_contain_unresolved_profile, no_require_profile_on_auth_commands.
No business commands exist yet — IRequireProfile will be applied as domain features are implemented (Step 13+)

Option A (MediatR pipeline behavior) chosen over Option B (middleware): clean integration with existing behavior chain, no ASP.NET dependencies in behavior, consistent with AuthorizationBehavior pattern
RequireProfileBehaviorTests: 7 unit tests — ProfileId=0 blocked, ProfileId<0 blocked, valid ProfileId passes, no-IRequireProfile always passes (3 InlineData variants)

step_log.description: Audit and confirm zero profile bypass patterns; update governance/docs to reflect Steps 11+12 completion
notes: AUDIT RESULT: Zero bypass patterns found in production code after Steps 11+12.
No ProfileId == 0 conditionals allowing execution. No COALESCE. No DefaultProfileId. No optional/nullable ProfileId parameters.
Single ProfileId source confirmed: ICurrentUser.ProfileId (from JWT claim via HttpContextCurrentUser) is the sole runtime source.

RefreshTokenHandler comment updated: pre-Step-11 tokens (V008 DEFAULT 0) pass ProfileId = 0 through rotation. This is safe — RequireProfileBehavior blocks all protected operations. No conditional bypass exists.
00_SYSTEM_RULES.json: VIOLATION-005, 006, 007 marked RESOLVED. enforcement_step updated to COMPLETE.
CODE-REVIEW.md: pipeline order updated (RequireProfileBehavior added), Profile hardening removed from 'Not Yet Implemented', VIOLATION-005/006/007 marked RESOLVED.
Profile hardening phase is now complete (Steps 11–13). Step 14 (align JWT/runtime profile source) is next.

step_log.description: Identity & Access foundation regression gate — full audit + documentation closeout
notes: REGRESSION AUDIT RESULT: Zero forbidden patterns in production code. Two migration comment matches (V005/V007) are historical only.
ICurrentUser is the sole runtime auth context. ITenantContext is a permitted thin delegate over ICurrentUser.CustomerId — no feature handler uses it yet.
No changes to production code required — foundation was already clean after Steps 11–13.
13_VALIDATION_RULES.json: icurrentuser_sole_auth_contract rule added.
CODE-REVIEW.md: section 9 'Identity & Access Foundation — Complete Criteria' added with invariants table.
DECISIONS.md: [2026-04-02] foundation closeout entry added at top.
Localization and downstream domains are UNBLOCKED. Next step: Step 15 — Languages table + service.

step_log.description: Profile model refactor — Profiles.UserId FK removed; access via ProfileUserMappings (many-to-many)
notes: SUPERSEDES step 11 note: 'Profile model confirmed: direct Profiles.UserId FK'. That decision is now REVERSED.
V009: CREATE ProfileUserMappings (ProfileId, UserId) PK; FKs to Profiles + Users. Backfill from Profiles.UserId. Drop FK. Drop Profiles.UserId column. Fully idempotent.
Three GetProfiles/GetAvailableProfiles SQL files rewritten: FROM Profiles p INNER JOIN ProfileUserMappings m ON m.ProfileId = p.Id WHERE m.UserId = @UserId AND p.CustomerId = @CustomerId.
AuthTestDataBuilder.InsertProfileAsync: Profiles INSERT no longer writes UserId column; mapping row written to ProfileUserMappings after profile insert.
AuthTestDataBuilder.InsertProfileUserMappingAsync(UserId, profileId) added for many-to-many test setup.
New tests (×2 per repository test file): ProfileExistsButNoMappingForUser_ReturnsEmpty, ProfileMappedToMultipleUsers_AllUsersCanAccess.
No handler C# code changes required — repositories unchanged in signature; SQL alone enforces access.
SelectProfileHandler access validation (profiles.FirstOrDefault) remains correct — mapping-join SQL guarantees only accessible profiles are returned.
